strategy_summary: The binary is vulnerable to a buffer overflow in the 'vulnerable_function' which reads 512 bytes into a 128-byte buffer. We can use this to overwrite the return address and control the execution flow. Since there is no PIE, we can directly use the addresses of the PLT and GOT entries. We will use ROP to call 'system' with the argument '/bin/sh'. We already have the offset and the pop_rdi_ret gadget, so we can proceed to craft the exploit.
